steps.title: Identify the noun and relative clause
steps.content:
The blank sits after the article a and before the relative clause who was not the first to construct the device, so it must be a noun that the relative clause describes.
Thus we are looking for a noun like “natural philosopher” that can be followed by that who‑clause.

steps.title: Apply punctuation rule for the appositive
steps.content:
The phrase in this case, Henry Cavendish is non‑restrictive extra information, so it needs matching delimiters on both sides.
Only choice D uses opening and closing dashes: natural philosopher—in this case, Henry Cavendish—, correctly setting off the appositive.
The other choices either use only one dash, a comma, or a colon, breaking the matching‑delimiter rule.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
